vulnerability.reason: Reference describes over-trust in visual saliency maps despite known faithfulness flaws; generated describes reliance forced by lack of model access, missing the 'over-trust despite known flaws' condition.
vulnerability.reference: Developer over-trust in visual explanation saliency maps despite known faithfulness flaws.
vulnerability.generated: Engineers lack direct access to model internals, forcing reliance on unverified post-hoc explanations without fidelity guarantees.

impact.reason: Both describe flawed logic/models persisting in production and propagating unreliable decisions at scale.
impact.reference: Flawed models persist in production, amplifying biased or unsafe decisions at scale.
impact.generated: Developers misattribute model errors, reinforcing flawed logic and propagating unreliable decisions into production systems.

dose_response.reason: Unfaithful explanations cause misattribution of errors, leading to propagation of flawed logic into production; the causal arc matches reference.
dose_response.reference: Unfaithful black-box explanations cause developers to misattribute errors and over-trust flawed attributions, resulting in biased models persisting and wasted debugging effort on spurious correlations.
dose_response.generated: Black-box explanation methods produce unfaithful, unstable, or non-robust explanations, and when used by engineers to debug models, trigger reliance on unverified post-hoc explanations without fidelity guarantees, leading to misattribution of errors and propagation of flawed logic into production.
